description_long: This object consists of three stylized mushrooms with large, flat, polygonal caps colored in a muted red-orange and adorned with irregular white spots. The caps are broad and slightly angular, giving a low-poly, geometric appearance. Each mushroom has a thick, short stem that is brown at the top and transitions to a lighter beige at the base, suggesting a natural, earthy texture. The mushrooms are grouped closely together, with varying sizes, evoking a cluster commonly found in parks or green urban spaces. The surface of the caps appears matte and slightly rough, with the white spots providing a distinct visual contrast. No gills are visible beneath the caps, and the stems are solid and robust, supporting the wide caps above.
description: A cluster of three stylized, red-orange mushrooms with white spots and thick, brown-beige stems. The caps are broad, flat, and polygonal, giving a geometric, low-poly look.
description_view_0: Three mushrooms of varying sizes are visible, showing the tops of their red-orange caps with white spots and thick stems.
description_view_1: The mushrooms are seen from a lower angle, emphasizing the height of the stems and the underside of the caps, though no gills are visible.
description_view_2: A close-up of the largest mushroom's cap and stem, with the other mushrooms partially visible in the background.
description_view_3: The cluster is viewed from the opposite side, highlighting the broad caps and the arrangement of the mushrooms in relation to each other.
